title: Meta stock sinks after Q1 earnings as company raises 2026 AI spending forecast to $125 billion-$145 billion
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Meta stock dropped significantly after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to $125-$145 billion, up from $115-$135 billion.
excerpt: Meta stock dropped significantly after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to $125-$145 billion, up from $115-$135 billion. This increase in projected spending led to a 10% decline in Meta's stock price.

content.introduction: Meta stock dropped significantly after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to $125-$145 billion, up from $115-$135 billion. This increase in projected spending led to a 10% decline in Meta's stock price.

Meta stock sinks after Q1 earnings as company raises 2026 AI spending forecast to $125 billion-$145 billion

Meta stock dropped 10% after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion, up from $115-$135 billion. This increase in capital expenditures raised concerns about the company's AI investments.

# Meta stock sinks after Q1 earnings as company raises 2026 AI spending forecast to $125 billion-$145 billion

Myles Udland

Myles Udland  · Head of News

Updated Thu, April 30, 2026 at 8:29 AM MDT 2 min read [...] Investors were also eagerly awaiting an update on Meta’s spending plans in the report, which showed overall company costs are expected to remain steady while its capital expenditures — which are more focused on AI-related investments — are expected to rise.

Meta stock fell as much as 10% in early trading on Thursday.

Meta said its 2026 capex spending will come in between $125 billion and $145 billion, up from its earlier forecast of $115 billion to $135 billion. Overall expenses are expected to remain the same, reaching a range of $162 billion to $169 billion for the year. Myles Udland  · Head of News

Meta stock fell as much as 10% in early trading on Thursday.

Meta said its 2026 capex spending will come in between $125 billion and $145 billion, up from its earlier forecast of $115 billion to $135 billion. Overall expenses are expected to remain the same, reaching a range of $162 billion to $169 billion for the year. ### Quick Read

   Meta Platforms (META) stock fell in early trading after the company beat Q1 FY2026 earnings but raised its 2026 CapEx guidance to $125–$145 billion, up $10 billion from January. 
   Meta Platforms’ capital spending intensity is rising faster than the company’s revenue, with Reality Labs burning $4.03 billion quarterly and AI ROI remaining unclear despite strong ad monetization. 
   The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and Meta wasn't one of them. Get them here FREE. [...] Shares of Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) are down roughly 8% to $614 in early trading on Thursday, April 30, after the social media giant paired a Q1 FY2026 earnings beat with a much bigger capital expenditure (CapEx) commitment for the year. The stock closed Wednesday at $669.12 and is indicated near $606.55 ahead of the open.

The selloff is striking given the headline numbers. Meta Platforms posted Q1 2026 revenue of $56.31 billion, advertising revenue of $55.02 billion, GAAP earnings per share (EPS) of $10.44, and operating income of $22.8 billion. [...] The catalyst sits one slide deeper in the release. Meta Platforms raised its full-year 2026 CapEx guidance to $125 billion to $145 billion, up $10 billion on both ends from the January range. Investors are now asking whether artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure spending is spiraling out of control.

_The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and Meta wasn't one of them._Get them here FREE.

## The CapEx Bombshell That Spooked Wall Street

The company cited higher component pricing and additional data center costs to support future year capacity for the increase. Meta Platforms' Q1 CapEx alone hit $18.99 billion, a 47% year-over-year (YoY) jump.

## Meta Stock Takes Hit From CapEx Increase

For the current quarter, Meta guided for sales of $59.5 billion, roughly even with analysts' Q2 forecasts ahead of the report.

But Meta raised its guidance for capital expenditures beyond Wall Street's forecasts. The company said it expects to spend $125 billion to $145 billion on capex this year. Zuckerberg told analysts in January that the company expects to spend $115 billion to $135 billion on 2026 capital expenditures. That's after spending about $70 billion in 2025, a total that was revised higher by Meta throughout the year.

The higher 2026 estimate reflects "expectations for higher component pricing this year and, to a lesser extent, additional data center costs to support future year capacity," the company said in its news release. [...] BREAKING: Fed's Inflation Gauge Hits Two-Year High, As Expected
